page_title: Wage overtime and meal break law — Mississippi
title_tag: Mississippi Wage, Overtime and Meal Break Law 2026 | Teamed
meta_description: Mississippi has no state minimum wage (federal $7.25/hr applies), no state overtime law, and no adult meal or rest break statute. Federal FLSA is the floor. EOR from $599/month.
h1: Does Mississippi have its own minimum wage, overtime, or meal break law?
target_keyword: Mississippi wage overtime meal break law 2026

hero_image.alt: Dollar sign and clock face on deep green background representing Mississippi wage and overtime law 2026.

provisional_facts.note: Post-litigation status as of 2026-05-30. Judicial rollback of 2024 DOL increases. Confirm before publishing.
Unchanged since 24 July 2009. No increase pending as of 2026-05-30.

provisional_facts.value: Miss. Code § 71-1-35 — twice per month for 50+ employees
provisional_facts.note: Confirm current text of § 71-1-35 as restaurant exemption clauses evolved pre-2014.

flags.note: The $684/week FLSA OT salary threshold is the post-litigation operative figure. The DOL's July 2024 ($844/week) and January 2025 ($1,128/week) increases were vacated by federal courts. The $684/week level from the 2019 final rule is the current operative floor as of 30 May 2026. Verify before publishing if more than 60 days have elapsed since build date.

Cache notes 'some restaurant exemptions' to Mississippi's payment frequency requirement. Page treats these as payment-timing-only, not a rate exemption. Confirm scope if a food-service employer queries this specifically.

review_queue_note: 2 provisional flags. Flag-001 (FLSA OT threshold litigation status) is medium-severity — verify before publishing if build is more than 60 days old.
